description: Mobile WebView attacks: Android WebView (addJavascriptInterface RCE, file:// access, mixed-content), iOS WKWebView (postMessage handler abuse, file:// URL load), Cordova/Ionic bridge abuse, Capacitor plugins. Reference-only — requires device + ability to control WebView source URL or postMessage. Maps to OWASP Mobile Top 10 2024 M4 + HackTricks Mobile WebView section.

addjavascriptinterface_rce.description: Android WebView with addJavascriptInterface() and target SDK <17 (or any version with @JavascriptInterface annotation on methods returning Java reflection access) → loaded JS can invoke any Java method. Reaches Runtime.exec → RCE inside app sandbox.

probes.description: Classic reflection chain — works when bridge object exposed without method allowlist. 'bridge' is whatever name addJavascriptInterface() registered.
Direct method invocation when bridge exposes shell-style helpers — happens in remote-config / debugging interfaces shipped to prod

webview_file_url_access.description: WebView with setAllowFileAccess(true) + setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs(true) — JS loaded from file:// can read every file in app sandbox via XHR. Pre-API-30 default = both true.

probes.description: Direct file:// load. Frida hook on WebView.loadUrl logs every call — if attacker can influence loadUrl param, exfil entire app sandbox.
JS payload running inside file:// origin reads arbitrary host files

wkwebview_message_handler.description: iOS WKWebView with WKScriptMessageHandler — JS posts via window.webkit.messageHandlers.<name>.postMessage(). If app dispatches messages back to native APIs without origin/auth validation, attacker JS triggers privileged native actions.

probes.description: Trigger native message handler with attacker payload — works if WKWebView source URL not locked to app-bundle scheme
Probe for exec-style handlers — happens in legacy hybrid apps that exposed dev tooling

wkwebview_universal_xss.description: WKWebView with WebKitWebProcessPlugInScriptWorld or WKUserContentController where attacker-loaded content escapes origin — CVE-2019-8506-class universal XSS. Mostly patched but still appears in pinned-WebKit-version apps.

probes.description: Test javascript: scheme handling in nested frame — should be blocked; if alert fires showing different origin, UXSS condition

cordova_ionic_plugin_abuse.description: Cordova/Ionic apps expose JS-callable plugins via cordova.exec(success,fail,'PluginName','method',[args]). Plugin allowlist in config.xml controls which plugins reachable from JS. Misconfigured wildcards allow plugin enumeration + abuse.

probes.description: Cordova File plugin — read arbitrary file if allowlist permissive
InAppBrowser plugin reaching javascript: URL — UXSS into app browser

capacitor_plugin_default.description: Capacitor exposes plugins under Capacitor.Plugins.<Name>. Plugins like Filesystem, App, Browser can be misused if the WebView allowed-origins list doesn't restrict third-party content.

probes.description: Capacitor Filesystem plugin call from attacker-loaded JS

webview_mixed_content_load.description: Android WebView with setMixedContentMode(MIXED_CONTENT_ALWAYS_ALLOW) — HTTPS app page loads HTTP subresources without TLS. Attacker on same network injects JS into the WebView's origin.

probes.description: If WebView loads HTTP script in HTTPS page, network attacker rewrites response to attacker JS

webview_screenshot_token_capture.description: WebView reflects bearer tokens / cookies into JS context. Attacker JS in WebView can read auth via document.cookie / localStorage / fetch('/me') — same-origin to app's API.

probes.description: JS in WebView origin → reads app's authenticated API + exfils. Possible whenever attacker controls any HTML loaded inside that WebView.
